title: ctenitis baulensis
intro: Ctenitis baulensis, also known as Baul's fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to New Zealand and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Ctenitis baulensis can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be dried and used in herbal remedies to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
